overall_theme: March 2026 is an exceptionally significant month due to a rare convergence of multiple long-range historical cycles (45, 60, 72, 90 years) all pointing to this period. The geopolitical trigger has already materialized with the US-Israel attack on Iran on Feb 28. Across all assets, the theme is heightened volatility and potential trend changes, with key weekly cycle convergences in weeks ending March 6 and March 27.

2026-03-02: S&P 500 (daily turning point, weekly cycle alignment)
2026-03-03: Gold (turning point, reinforced by daily dates March 3-5)
2026-03-04: EUR/USD (turning point, strong daily dates March 4-5)
2026-03-06: Bitcoin (turning point + daily TP + weekly cycle end)
2026-03-30: EUR/USD (turning point, end of month + weekly cycle alignment)

sp500.bias: Seasonally positive month, but multiple long-range cycle convergence and deteriorating technical indicators suggest caution
sp500.critical_window: Mid-month March 11-13 where daily and weekly cycles converge, and late month March 27 with negative statistical bias
top_insights: Rare convergence of 45, 60, 72, and 90-year historical cycles all pointing to March 2026
Geopolitical trigger materialized: US-Israel attack on Iran Feb 28
Eight consecutive months of gains with D9 indicator extreme reading (8-9) historically precedes corrections
Primary trend remains upward but quality of rally is weakening - corrections of 10-15% do not negate bullish primary trend
CNN Fear & Greed Index at 40 (Fear zone)
sp500.summary: Exceptionally significant month due to multi-cycle convergence. Primary trend up but correction increasingly likely. Key dates: March 2, 12, 18, 27.

bitcoin.bias: Neutral to slightly negative seasonally (53% negative over 15 years). Primary trend has shifted to bearish after breaking $70K-$78K support
bitcoin.critical_window: Weeks ending March 6 and March 27 are the two most important weekly cycle windows
top_insights: 35-month cycle of gains completed October 2025, ~52% decline from peak since then
Break below $70K-$78K support is structural, not temporary
Any rally should be treated as correction within developing bearish primary trend
Symmetrical triangle pattern forming suggests continued downward pressure
bitcoin.summary: Bitcoin in bearish primary trend after breaking critical $70K-$78K support. Treat rallies as corrections. Key weekly cycles: weeks ending March 6 and March 27.

eurusd.bias: Seasonally mildly positive for Euro, but monthly cycle active this month complicates the picture
eurusd.critical_window: Week ending March 13 is the strongest weekly cycle - convergence with monthly cycle and daily turning points raises probability of significant decision point at mid-month
top_insights: Breakout above 1.1920 proved to be failed breakout as predicted - weekly falling star candle confirmed
Break below 1.1768 with force indicates bears in control short-term
Direction chosen is downward for at least 1-2 weeks
Monthly and weekly cycle convergence at mid-month raises probability of significant decision point
eurusd.summary: Euro in weaker phase after failed breakout above 1.1920 and break below 1.1768. Short-term bias downward. Key window: week ending March 13 (strongest cycle convergence).

gold.bias: Seasonally one of the weakest months for Gold, but no monthly cycles this month shifts focus to weekly/daily patterns
gold.critical_window: Weeks ending March 6 and March 27 are the two primary weekly cycle windows. High density of daily turning points means sharp volatility spikes
support: 4381 (critical - above = correction, below = trend expansion to $3,886)
top_insights: Central peak arrived at Jan 29 turning point, confirming cyclical analysis
Elliott Wave 5 may have completed at the peak
$4,381 is the critical level: above = correction in uptrend, below = trend expansion toward $3,886
High density of daily turning points this month means not only reversals but also sharp volatility spikes
Resistance at $5,100-$5,598 could bring seller pressure from stuck longs and shorts
gold.summary: Gold in seasonally weak month with structurally complex picture. Critical level $4,381: above = bullish correction, below = bearish expansion. High daily TP density = elevated volatility.

oil.bias: Seasonally positive - all three decade patterns support upward movement, but high volatility expected
oil.critical_window: Two very strong weekly cycles in weeks ending March 20 and March 27, with daily dates around March 16, 20, 26-27 providing convergence
top_insights: $55.36-$56.00 multi-month support has held firmly
Break above $64.40-$66.50 resistance on March 1 catalyzed by Iran attack
Until Oil reclaims $70.00+ and confirms above $78.90, upside remains correction within bearish structure
Geopolitical catalyst (Iran/Venezuela) explains timing but chart explains why the move was possible
In Oil, meaningful changes arrive from time zones where everyone feels secure
oil.summary: Seasonally positive with $55-$56 support holding and $64-$66 breakout. But true trend reversal requires $70+ and $78.90 confirmation. Key cycles: weeks ending March 20 and 27.

cross_asset_themes: Geopolitical Catalyst Materialized: US-Israel attack on Iran Feb 28 was forecast by cycle analysis in the annual 2026 report. This has immediately impacted Oil (upward gap), Gold (safe haven flows), and S&P 500 (risk-off pressure). The multi-cycle convergence (45, 60, 72, 90 years) identified this as a sensitive geopolitical window.

End-of-Month Multi-Asset Convergence March 26-27: Four out of five assets (Bitcoin, Gold, Oil, S&P 500) have turning point dates on March 26-27, all aligning with weekly cycle endings. This is the highest-density multi-asset convergence window of the month.

Early-Month Cluster March 2-6: S&P 500 (March 2), Bitcoin (March 2, 6), Gold (March 3-5), EUR/USD (March 4-5), and Oil (March 4) all have turning points in the first week, many reinforced by the weekly cycle ending March 6.

Mid-Month Volatility Window March 12-17: S&P 500 (March 12, 17-18), EUR/USD (March 12-13, strongest weekly cycle), Gold (March 13), Bitcoin (March 16-17, high volatility), and Oil (March 16) create a dense mid-month cluster.

Primary Trend Exhaustion Theme: S&P 500 showing 8-month rally exhaustion with D9 extreme, Bitcoin has broken below primary trend support ($70K-$78K), EUR/USD failed breakout above 1.1920, Gold potentially completing Elliott Wave 5 at the peak. Multiple assets showing signs of primary trend exhaustion simultaneously.

Historical Cycle Parallels: March 2026 aligns with major historical events across 45-year (1981 Reagan), 60-year (1966 Vietnam), 72-year (1954 hydrogen bomb), and 90-year (1936 Nazi remilitarization) cycles - all periods of geopolitical escalation and market turbulence.
risk_management_rules: Maximum risk per futures trade: 1% of account. No exceptions.
Maintain 30-40% liquidity in portfolios during this period of heightened uncertainty.
Do not hold open positions over weekends without stops - one event outside trading hours can open with an aggressive gap.
Stop loss must be set within the system with clear risk defined from the outset.
All turning point dates may deviate by +/- 1 day from the marked date - be prepared.
When a double cycle appears (two dates close together), the first typically triggers a direction change and the second reinforces the original trend direction.
Do not pre-mark turning dates as high or low - direction is only known when observing price movement into the cycle date.
Do not buy blindly at support - check trading cycles, whether decline is controlled or panic, and whether there is loss of control.
In stocks, prefer names that have already corrected 10-15% and examine them at support levels.
Not every decline is an opportunity - sometimes it is just the beginning.
A 10-15% correction does not negate a positive primary trend - it restores balance and reduces risk.
In this type of month, the difference between a trader who preserves capital and one who tries to catch every move is the difference between surviving and rebuilding.
2026 was described in the annual report as a psychologically very sensitive year for markets.
Stay connected to the Discord community for real-time updates as conditions change dynamically.
All information is for educational and learning purposes only - not investment advice.
